duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: claim() calls pool.collect(type(uint128).max) sweeping ALL fees from the shared Uniswap V3 position, stealing other users' uncollected fees (sent to FEE_TAKER) and permanently DoSing subsequent claims via underflow
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All 9 threats describe the identical shared-position fee collection bug in ILOPool.claim() where collect(max) drains all owed tokens and the remainder is sent to FEE_TAKER, causing underflow/revert for later claimers. ECON-H001 selected for its comprehensive economics, Solidity 0.7.6 unchecked arithmetic detail, and impact assessment.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: Whitelist checkWhitelist() logic flaw: default _whitelistIndex[to]==0 for unwhitelisted addresses bypasses the `_whitelistIndex[to] > _allowedWhitelistIndex` revert when _allowedWhitelistIndex > 0
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All 9 threats describe the identical logic flaw in Whitelist.sol checkWhitelist() at lines 152-154 where Solidity default mapping value 0 causes the OR condition to be false for unwhitelisted users. TECH-H001 selected for the clearest technical explanation with precise preconditions and investigation rationale.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: Per-user cap bypass via NFT transfer: balanceOf(recipient)==0 check allows minting a new position with reset raiseAmount after transferring the existing NFT away
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All 10 threats target the same root cause: ILOPool.buy() enforces maxCapPerUser per-NFT-position rather than per-address cumulatively, and ERC721 transferability resets balanceOf to 0. ECON2-H012 specifically describes using Multicall for atomic execution but the underlying code defect is the same cap-per-position design. ECON-H003 selected for comprehensive attack economics and victim impact analysis.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: Missing validation that initialPoolPriceX96 < sqrtRatioUpperX96 in ILOManager.initILOPool() allows creation of pools with price outside the concentrated liquidity range, breaking all liquidity math
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All 8 threats describe the identical missing upper bound check in ILOManager.sol initILOPool() RANGE require statement. TECH-H004 selected for the most detailed technical explanation of how the incomplete validation affects getLiquidityForAmount calculations in buy() and launch().

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: Permissionless initProject() allows anyone to claim project admin for any token pair by calling first, permanently locking out the legitimate project team
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All 4 threats describe the same permissionless first-come-first-served admin assignment in ILOManager.initProject(). TECH2-H006 refers to the second entry with that ID (about permissionless initProject). ECON-H008 selected for detailed economic impact analysis including vesting config manipulation scenarios.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: buy(raiseAmount, recipient) allows third-party griefing by filling a whitelisted user's maxCapPerUser allocation without their consent, since payer and recipient are separated
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All 7 threats describe the same griefing vector where msg.sender pays but recipient's cap is consumed in ILOPool.buy(). TECH2-H006 refers to the first entry with that ID (about griefing). ECON-H006 selected for combined economic analysis showing how this pairs with cap bypass for monopolization.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: Zero-duration vesting schedule (start==end) causes permanent division-by-zero in _unlockedLiquidity via FullMath.mulDiv with zero denominator, permanently DoSing all claims
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All 10 threats describe the same missing start < end validation in ILOVest._validateVestSchedule() leading to division by zero in the else branch of _unlockedLiquidity(). TECH2-H007 has two entries with same ID, both about this vulnerability. TECH-H007 selected for the most precise technical walkthrough of the exact code path and branching conditions.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: TWAP oracle fallback to near-spot price on newly created pools (short observation history) enables price manipulation to bypass Whitelist _maxAddressCap
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All 9 threats describe the same oracle vulnerability where UniswapV3Oracle.peek() falls back to longestPeriod (potentially seconds) for new pools, making TWAP manipulable. TECH2-H005 refers to the first entry with that ID (about oracle manipulation). ECON-H007 selected for detailed flash loan economics and cap bypass profit analysis.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: Reentrancy window in ILOPool.launch() where _launchSucceeded is set after external calls (_refundProject transfer and addLiquidity), potentially allowing state machine invariant violation
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All 4 threats describe the same checks-effects-interactions violation where _launchSucceeded = true is the last statement in launch(), after _refundProject's external SALE_TOKEN transfer. TECH-H009 selected for the most comprehensive technical description of the reentrancy path and state corruption scenarios.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: Liquidity accounting mismatch between per-user floor-division calculations in buy() and aggregate calculation in launch() causes sum of individual liquidities to differ from actual Uniswap position liquidity
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All 5 threats describe the same rounding discrepancy where buy() calculates getLiquidity(raiseAmount_i) * shares / BPS per user while launch() calculates getLiquidity(totalRaised) in aggregate. TECH-H010 selected for detailed technical explanation of the non-linearity and accumulated floor division effects.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: ILOPool.buy() calls tokenOfOwnerByIndex() (ERC721Enumerable function) but contract inherits ERC721 only, potentially bricking repeat purchases or causing inconsistent tokenId resolution
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: Both threats describe the same missing ERC721Enumerable inheritance issue. TECH2-H003 has a colliding ID with a different vulnerability (cap bypass) in another entry; the second entry with that ID matches this group. TECH-H012 selected for more detailed analysis and deployment assumptions.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: Whitelist.receive() refunds ETH via transfer() with 2300 gas stipend, causing DoS for contract wallets attempting self-whitelist
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: Both threats describe the same 2300 gas stipend limitation in Whitelist.receive(). TECH2-H005 has a colliding ID with a different vulnerability (oracle manipulation) in another entry; the second entry with that ID matches this group. TECH-H014 selected for more detailed preconditions and deployment context.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: Multicall delegatecall-to-self enables atomic state interleaving that may bypass per-call invariant assumptions around lifecycle flags
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: Both threats describe the same generic multicall composability risk via delegatecall to address(this). TECH2-H004 has a colliding ID with a different vulnerability (upper price validation) in another entry; the second entry with that ID matches this group. TECH-H015 selected for more detailed investigation of lifecycle flag interactions.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: Whitelist and contribution cap checks only apply to transfers from _pool; direct peer-to-peer or alternative DEX transfers completely bypass all restrictions including blacklist
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All 3 threats describe the same design limitation where checkWhitelist() only enforces restrictions when from==_pool, allowing secondary transfers to bypass all whitelist/blacklist controls. ECON-H016 selected as the most comprehensive description with clear attack economics.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: Fee-on-transfer RAISE_TOKEN causes accounting insolvency: buy() records nominal raiseAmount but contract receives less, making last refunders unable to claim full amounts
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All 3 threats describe the same fee-on-transfer token incompatibility where buy() records raiseAmount without checking actual balance delta, creating refund insolvency. TECH2-H008 selected for the clearest description of both buy() and claimRefund() impact.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: ILOManager.launch() requires exact sqrtPriceX96 match with initialPoolPriceX96 on a publicly tradable Uniswap V3 pool, enabling anyone to grief launch indefinitely by moving the price
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: Both threats describe the same exact-price-match griefing vector in ILOManager.launch() where any swap moving the pool price blocks the launch. ECON-H005 selected for comprehensive economic analysis including minimal attack cost and extortion scenarios.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: UniswapV3Oracle.peek() with zero observation history causes period=0, making OracleLibrary.consult() revert with 'BP' and DoSing all whitelist-period pool buys
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: Both threats describe the same oracle DoS when getOldestObservationSecondsAgo returns 0, causing consult() to revert and blocking all token transfers during the whitelist period. OPS-H020 selected for clearer description of the failure chain from oracle to checkWhitelist to transfer hook.
